question: Consider the following statements: 1. The Governor of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is appointed by the Central Government. 2. Certain provisions in the Constitution of India give the Central Government the right to issue directions to the RBI in public interest. 3. The Governor of the RBI draws his power from the RBI Act. Which of the above statements are correct?
Which one of the following statements correctly describes the meaning of legal tender money ?

options.a: The money which is tendered in courts of law to defray the fee of legal cases
options.b: The money which a creditor is under compulsion to accept in settlement of his claims
options.c: The bank money in the form of cheques, drafts, bills of exchange, etc.
options.d: The metallic money in circulation in a country

question: With reference to the Indian economy, what are the advantages of "Inflation- Indexed Bonds (IIBs)"? 1. Government can reduce the coupon rates on its borrowing by way of IIBs. 2. IIBs provide protection to the investors from uncertainty regarding inflation. 3. The interest received as well as capital gains on IIBs are not taxable. Which of the statements given above are correct?

Consider the following statements : The RBI’s recent directives relating to 'Storage of Payment System Data', command the payment system providers that 1. they shall ensure that entire data relating to payment systems operated by them are stored in a system only in India 2. they shall ensure that the systems are owned and operated by public sector enterprises 3.they shall submit the consolidated system audit report to the CAG Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

With reference to Central Bank digital currencies, consider the following statements: 1. It is possible to make payments in a digital currency without using the US dollar or SWIFT system. 2. A digital currency can be distributed with a condition programmed into it such as a time-frame for spending it. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

Consider the following statements in respect of the digital rupee: 1. It is sovereign currency issued by the RBI in alignment with its monetary policy. 2. It appears as a liability on the RBI's balance sheet. 3. It is insured against inflation by its very design. 4. It is freely convertible against commercial bank money and cash. Which of the statements given above are correct?

With respect to framework for the unclaimed Deposits, consider the following statements: 1. The unclaimed deposits are transferred by the Bank to the Deposit Education and Awareness fund maintained with the Ministry of Finance. 2. The depositors or their nominees cannot claim these deposits after a fixed period of time. 3. The depositors or their nominees can claim these deposits through the UDGAM Portal. Which among the statements given above is/are correct?
Which among the following best describes the concept of “Unclaimed Deposits”, recently in news?

options.a: Deposits that are not claimed by the depositors within 10 years and hence become the property of banks.
options.b: Deposits that lapse automatically after a 10 years of inactivity and become the property of Government.
options.c: Deposits in inactive bank accounts where withdrawals are prohibited
options.d: Deposits that are not claimed by the depositors within 10 years, but the legal ownership continues to vest with the depositor.

question: Consider the following statements: Statement-I: The Banking Laws (Amendment) Act, 2025 permits depositors to nominate up to 4 persons for bank deposits through simultaneous or successive nomination, whereas in the case of bank lockers, only successive nomination is permitted. Statement-II: Simultaneous nomination for deposits enables depositors to distribute the deposit amount among multiple nominees in predefined proportions, thereby reducing disputes and delays in settlement. Statement-III: Only successive nomination has been permitted for bank lockers because the contents of lockers are not documented making simultaneous nomination operationally complex. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

question: Consider the following statements with respect to Payment Regulatory Board (PRB): 1. The PRB is established under the provisions of RBI act, 1934 based upon recommendations of Ratan Watal Committee. 2. The PRB is completely independent from RBI. 3. The decisions of the PRB is based upon majority. 4. The Governor of the RBI enjoys veto power in the decisions of PRB. Which among the statements given above is/are incorrect?

With reference to Central Bank digital currencies, consider the following statements: 1. It is possible to make payments in a digital currency without using the US dollar or SWIFT system. 2. A digital currency can be distributed with a condition programmed into it such as a time-frame for spending it. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

Consider the following statements in respect of the digital rupee: 1. It is sovereign currency issued by the in alignment with its monetary policy. 2. It appears as a liability on the RBI's balance sheet. 3. It is insured against inflation by its very design. 4. It is freely convertible against commercial bank money and cash. Which of the statements given above are correct?

Consider the following statements related to Retail CBDC: 1. The Retail CBDC wallet can be provided by both banks and Non-Banks. 2. Presently, there is no need for Bank account for opening and operating CBDC wallet. 3. Retail CBDC can be transferred to someone’s Bank Account. How many statements given above is/are correct?

With reference to Wholesale CBDC, consider the following statements: 1. The Wholesale CBDC is a token based CBDC. 2. The Wholesale CBDC can be used only by selected Financial Institutions. 3. Presently, the wholesale CBDC can be used for making payments in the primary market for buying G-Secs from RBI. How many statements given above is/are correct?

Consider the following statements: Statement-I: The Unified Market Interface is a proposed digital platform developed by RBI for trading and settlement of tokenised financial market instruments using wholesale central bank digital currency. Statement-II: Settlement through wholesale CBDC in the Unified Market Interface enables delivery-versus-payment and hence addresses delays in settlement. Statement-III: The Unified Market Interface is intended to benefit retail investors who invest in financial instruments such as Bonds, G-Secs, Corporate Bonds etc. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

question: The “Project M-Bridge”, sometime, seen in news, is

options.a: Initiative of RBI to facilitate cross border instant payments
options.b: Initiative of RBI to introduce interoperability between CBDC and UPI
options.c: China-led Initiative to facilitate cross border payment through CBDC
options.d: Initiative of Bank of International settlement (BIS) to facilitate cross border payment through payment systems such as UPI.

question: Which among the following projects can be financed from the Sovereign Green Bonds? 1. Promote public transportation including its electrification. 2. Promoting water efficient irrigation systems 3. Nuclear Power 4. Large Hydropower Projects How many statements given above is/are correct?

With respect to Sovereign Green Bonds, consider the following statements: 1. The proceeds of the Sovereign Green Bonds are maintained in a separate fund under the Public account of India. 2. Non-Resident investors are allowed to invest in Sovereign Green Bonds without any limit. 3. These Bonds are eligible to be considered as SLR Securities. 4. Unlike normal Bonds, Investors enjoy exemption from Capital Gains Tax on Sovereign Green Bonds (SGBs) How many statements given above is/are correct?

How many among the following is/are associated with the Greenium in the Sovereign Green Bonds? 1. Higher Returns for the Investors 2. Lower Interest Burden on the Government 3. Higher Demand for the Bonds Select the correct answer using the code given below:
A lower Greenium on the Green Bonds imply which among the following?

options.a: Lower Bond Prices of Green Bonds as compared to Normal Bonds
options.b: Higher Demand for Green Bonds as compared to Normal Bonds
options.c: Lower difference in the yield rate between Green Bonds and Normal Bonds
options.d: Higher difference in the yield rate between

question: How many of the below mentioned facilities is/are provided by the RBI through the E-Kuber Platform? 1. Auction of the G-Secs to all the investors, including Retail Investors. 2. Enable Banks to maintain CRR Deposits with the RBI. 3. Enable Government to borrow through Ways and Means Advances (WMAs) Select the correct answer using the code given below:

With reference to Retail Direct Scheme, consider the following statements: 1. The Retail Direct scheme enables Financial Institutions such as Banks to buy G-Secs from the RBI on the behalf of Retail Investors. 2. The Retail Direct Platform provides for Trading of the G-Secs in the secondary market. 3. Non-Resident Investors are not allowed to invest under the scheme. How many statements given above is/are correct?

Consider the following statements related to Credit Information Companies: 1. Credit Information Bureau India Limited (CIBIL) is a Government owned Credit Information company. 2. All the Banks must become members of all Credit information Companies. 3. Individuals can access their credit report from the CICs free of cost once in a year. 4. Under RBI’s guidelines, Banks are prohibited from given loans to individuals whose credit score is below a certain level. How many statements given above is/are correct?

In line with the recommendations of Bimal Jalan Committee on RBI’s Economic Capital Framework, which among the following can be considered as sources of Realized profits of RBI and hence transferrable to the Government? 1. Interest on loans given by RBI to Banks 2. Appreciation in Foreign Currency with RBI 3. Appreciation in value of Gold in custody of RBI 4. Interest on G-Secs in custody of RBI Select the correct answer using the code given below:

Consider the following statements: Statement-I: The RBI has transferred record surplus of more than Rs 2 lakh crores as dividend to the Government in 2025. Statement-II: The RBI has reviewed its economic capital framework and reduced the threshold for the Contingency risk buffer in 2025. Statement-III: The RBI has made profits by selling dollars to control Rupee Depreciation. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements? Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

question: Consider the following statements related to Deposit Insurance in India: 1. Deposits maintained with NBFCs and Rural Cooperative Societies are not insured. 2. Deposits of Foreign Governments with Banks are not insured. 3. Deposit insurance can be increased by depositing funds in different accounts of the Banks held in different capacity and different right. 4. More than 90% of Bank accounts are fully insured in India. Which among the statements given above is/are correct?

Which among the following is/are advantages of Public Tech Platform for Frictionless Credit? 1. This platform enables Banks and NBFCs to disburse loans directly to borrowers. 2. Account aggregators would no longer be needed in India. 3. Better credit risk appraisal and lower NPAs of Banks 4. Reduced dependence on Informal sources for borrowing Select the correct answer using the code given below:

Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)? 1. It decides the RBI's benchmark interest rates. 2. It is a 12-member body including the Governor of RBI and is reconstituted every year. 3. It functions under the chairmanship of the Union Finance Minister. Select the correct answer using the code given below :

With respect to Inflation Targeting Framework in India, consider the following statements: 1. The Inflation Targeting framework has been provided statutory recognition under the RBI Act, 1934. 2. The Inflation Target is fixed by the RBI once in every 5 years. 3. The Inflation targets are specified in terms of Consumer Price Index (CPI). Which among statements given above is/are correct?

Consider the following statements with respect to Inflation Targeting Framework: 1. The RBI has always been able to keep the inflation under the target since its introduction in 2016. 2. So far, the MPC has taken all the decisions based on consensus without recourse to voting. 3. If the RBI fails to maintain inflation withing the target for 3 consecutive months, it is required to submit report to the Government. Which among the statements given above is/are incorrect?

Consider the following statements: Statement-I: Under Variable Rate Repos, RBI gives short-term loans to the Banks at prevailing Repo rate. Statement-II: The Variable Rate Repos are used to manage short term liquidity in the economy. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I
options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I
options.c: Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
options.d: Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

question: Which among the following is/are the likely impacts of Foreign Exchange Sell/Buy Swap carried out by RBI? 1. Increase in Forex Reserves 2. Rupee Devaluation 3. Check Exchange Rate Volatility Select the correct answer using the code given below:

Which among the following measures is/are likely to be taken to check Rupee Depreciation? 1. Curb import of non-essential items 2. Encourage issuance of Masala Bonds 3. Adopt Forex Sell/Buy Swap 4. Adopt Forex Buy/Sell Swap Select the correct answer using the code given below:

Which among the following is likely to lead to increase in money supply in the economy? 1. Increase in Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) rates. 2. Forex Buy/Sell Swap 3. Sale of G-Secs under Open Market Operations (OMOs) Select the correct answer using the code given below:
Which among the following objectives can be simultaneously pursued through the adoption of Forex Sell/Buy Swap?

options.a: Control Rupee Appreciation and Expansionary Policy
options.b: Control Rupee Appreciation and Contractionary Policy
options.c: Control Rupee Depreciation and Expansionary Policy
options.d: Control Rupee Depreciation and Contractionary Policy

question: When the Reserve Bank of India reduces the Statutory Liquidity Ratio by 50 basis points which of the following is likely to happen?

options.a: India's GDP growth rate increases drastically.
options.b: Foreign Institutional Investors may bring more capital into our country.
options.c: Scheduled Commercial Banks may cut their lending rates.
options.d: It may drastically reduce the liquidity to the banking system.

question: Consider the following scenario: The banks in India are taking more loans from the RBI and depositing less money with the RBI under Reverse Repo window. What could be the cause for such a scenario?

options.b: Decrease in Credit Creation by Banks
options.c: Decrease in Demand for Currency by Public

question: Which among the following is likely to decrease Banking System Liquidity in Indian Economy? 1. Increase in Demand for Currency 2. Decrease in Credit Deposit Ratio 3. RBI’s Intervention to control Rupee Depreciation. Select the correct answer using the code given below:

Consider the following statements: Statement-I: In the recent past, the Banking system liquidity in the Indian Economy has turned into deficit. Statement-II: The Credit-Deposit Ratio of the Indian Banks has increased in the recent past. Statement-III: Th decrease in Banking system liquidity leads to increase in the rate of Interest on loans. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements? Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I

question: Consider the following statements with respect to Credit Deposit ratio (CDR): 1. An increase in loans relative to deposit leads to increase in Credit Deposit ratio. 2. An increase in preference for shares and mutual funds relative to deposits could lead to decrease in Credit Deposit Ratio. 3. The CDR cannot be more than 100%. Select the correct answer using the code given below:

Consider the following statements: Statement-I: The Credit-Deposit ratio of the Banks has increased in 2025-26. Statement-II: There has been shift in the nature of financial savings of the households from deposits towards mutual funds and capital market instruments. Statement-III: There has been shift in the funding of loans from deposits to equity capital of the Banks. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

question: Consider the following statements: Statement-I: The RBI has raised concerns over increase in Credit-Deposit Ratio of the Banks. Statement-II: Increase in Credit-Deposit ratio leads to liquidity risk and credit risk to the Banks. Statement-III: Increase in CDR leads to increase in Net Interest Margin (NIM) of Banks Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements? options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

question: Consider the following pairs: Ratio Meaning 1. Currency Deposit ratio Ratio of Currency with Public to Deposits with Banks 2. Cash Deposit ratio Ratio of Cash in hand with Banks and CRR Balances with RBI to Deposits 3. Investment Deposit Ratio Ratio of Loans to total Deposits Which among the above pairs is/are correctly matched?
An increase in Investment Deposit ratio of the Banks necessarily implies

options.a: Increase in loans of the Banks
options.b: Higher share of G-Secs in the Statutory Liquidity ratio (SLR)
options.c: Increase in Investment in the G-Secs by the Banks
options.d: Increase in Cash Reserve ratio (CRR) balances with the RBI.

question: With reference to Cash Deposit ratio of the Banks, consider the following statements: 1. The Cash Deposit Ratio is always equal to or higher than Cash reserve ratio of the Bank. 2. A Bank with higher Cash Deposit ratio is likely to meet its short-term obligations towards Depositors. Which among the statements given above is/are correct?
Which among the following best describes the concept of "Lazy Banking", sometimes seen in news?

options.a: Increase in NPAs due to excessive lending to less credit worthy borrowers.
options.b: Scenario wherein Banks invest more money in Government Bonds rather than giving loans to different sectors.
options.c: Scenario wherein Banks show eagerness to sell their NPAs to Bad bank rather than recovering NPAs on their own.
options.d: Scenario wherein Banks take more haircut under the IBC to resolve the cases expeditiously.

question: What is/are the purpose/purposes of the `Marginal Cost of Funds based Lending Rate (MCLR)’ announced by RBI? 1. These guidelines help improve the transparency in the methodology followed by banks for determining the interest rates on advances. 2. These guidelines help ensure availability of bank credit at interest rates which are fair to the borrowers as well as the banks. Select the correct answer using the code given below.

With reference to External Benchmarking of Loans, consider the following statements: 1. The External Benchmarking has been mandated to be adopted for both Fixed rate and Floating rate loans. 2. The Interest rate under the External Benchmark should be reset once in every 3 months. 3. Banks have discretion to fix their own spread. Which among the statements given above is/are correct?

With respect to External benchmarking of loans, consider the following statements: 1. The rate of interest on floating rate loans would be linked only to the Repo rate. 2. This regime has completely replaced the Marginal Cost of Lending Rate (MCLR) regime. Which among the statements given above is/are correct?

The Fixed rate loans are linked to MCLR while the floating rate loans are linked to External Benchmarking regime 3. The borrowers are not allowed to switch from floating rate loans to fixed rate loans Which among the statements given above is/are correct?

Indian Government Bond Yields are influenced by which of the following? 1. Actions of the United States Federal Reserve 2. Actions of the Reserve Bank of India 3. Inflation and short-term interest rates Select the correct answer using the code given below.

Consider the following statements: Statement-I: In the post-pandemic recent past, many Central Banks worldwide, had carried out interest rate hikes. Statement-II: Central Banks generally assume that they have the ability to counteract the rising consumer prices via monetary policy means. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I
options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I
options.c: Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
options.d: Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

question: Consider the following statements: Statement-I: Recently, the spread between US Government bond yields and Indian Bond Yields has reduced to 20-year low. Statement-II: The Bond Yields in USA has increased due to downgrade in Sovereign credit ratings while Bond Yields in India has decreased due to strong macro-economic fundamentals. Statement-III: The decrease in spread between US Government Bond yields and Indian Bond Yields generally leads to outflow of FPI from India. options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

question: Consider the following statements: Statement-I: Germany has replaced Japan as the world’s largest creditor nation after more than 3 decades. Statement-II: There has been increase in Bond Yields in Japan leading to large scale foreign outflows from Japan to other countries. options.a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I.
options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I.
options.c: Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
options.d: Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct.

question: What is the importance of the term "Interest Coverage Ratio" of a firm in India? 1. It helps in understanding the present risk of a firm that a bank is going to give loan to. 2. question: Statement-I: Syndicated lending spreads the risk of borrower default across multiple lenders. Statement-II: The syndicated loan can be a fixed amount/lump sum of funds, but cannot be a credit line. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

question: In the context of inflation, the momentum effect reflects/captures the:

options.a: the impact of price levels from the previous year’s base period on the current year-on-year inflation calculation.
options.b: the inflationary pressure caused by an increase in the speed at which money circulates in the economy.
options.c: the sudden spikes in food and fuel prices driven specifically by supply-side shocks.
options.d: month-on-month price changes in the current year. • Momentum effect captures the month-on-month price changes in the current year • Base effect reflects the influence of price movements in the corresponding months of the previous year on year-on-year inflation.

question: Consider the below statement: “The high Base effect has played a dominant role in keeping the inflation lower in the current year despite high momentum effect”. What does this statement mean?

options.a: Low prices in the corresponding month of previous year has kept the current year inflation lower despite increase in prices.
options.b: High prices in corresponding month of previous year has kept the current year inflation lower despite decrease in prices.
options.c: High prices in corresponding month of previous year has kept the current year inflation lower despite increase in prices.
options.d: Low prices in the corresponding month of previous year has kept the current year inflation lower despite decrease in prices.

options.d: Participatory Note SEBI’s Guidelines on Offshore Derivative Instruments (ODIs) 2 categories of FPI: • Category-I: Sovereign Wealth Funds, Government agencies, Central banks, Insurance, Pension Fund, Mutual Fund companies from FATF member countries. • Category-II: FPIs not registered under Category-I FPI, including individuals. Who can issue ODIs?: Only Category-I FPIs registered with SEBI. Do ODI Investor hold securities directly? No. FPI remains the owner of underlying security, but economic benefits are transferred to investor. Controversy: P-Notes can be used for money laundering through Round Tripping. Criteria for issuing ODIs: • FPIs must fulfill KYC norms to reduce anonymity of investors. • Monthly reporting of issuance of P-Notes Can P-Notes be traded outside India? Yes. But only the among the entities based in FATF member countries. SEBI’s Guidelines (2024) • FPIs issuing P-Notes shall have special type of registration with term “ODI” as suffix. • FPI cannot issue P-Notes against derivatives.

This agreement has incorporated special and differential treatment for the benefit of LDCs and developing countries. 4. The agreement has entered into force in 2025 after being ratified by 2/3 of member countries. Which among the statements given above is/are correct?
